stories.title: Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
stories.description: As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

stories.title: Iran's cultural heritage sites are being damaged by American and Israeli strikes
stories.description: The costs of the joint U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to some of Iran's most famous and beloved cultural heritage sites.

stories.title: 'Everybody was wearing black.' How the Iranian diaspora is observing Nowruz amid war
stories.description: Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.

stories.title: How the Iran war threatens global food supply
stories.description: About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped through the Strait and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.

stories.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
stories.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
stories.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

stories.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
stories.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Strike hits key nuclear site in Iran
stories.description: Hoda Abdel-Hamid is live from Doha, Qatar.

stories.title: Washington sends conflicting signals as war rages on in the Middle East
stories.description: As the Iran conflict enters its fourth week, strikes on nuclear sites and a reported attack on a US-UK base signal further escalation. With no clear exit in sight, fears are mounting of wider instability and humanitarian fallout. “The attack on Diego Garcia is an escalation… it’s...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Jerusalem Old City targeted by Iranian projectile
stories.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz warned that the United States and Israel would intensify their strikes on Iran in the coming week starting Sunday.

stories.title: Syrian Kurds return home to celebrate Nowruz for the first time since exile
stories.description: Nowruz, the Farsi-language word for “new year,” is an ancient Persian festival that is also celebrate by Kurds in Syria, Turkey and Iraq as well as Iran. It is characterized by colorful street festivals and torch-bearing processions winding their way into the mountains.

stories.title: US to send more Marines to Middle East as Trump hints at wind-down
stories.description: Three weeks into an escalating war in the Middle East, Iran threatened to expand it's retaliatory attacks to include recreational and tourist sites worldwide, as the US announced it was sending more warships and Marines to the region.

stories.title: How Iran's Revolutionary Guards helped Hezbollah prepare for its latest war with Israel
stories.description: With Lebanese Shiite group Hezbollah's military command reeling from Israel's brutal 2024 offensive that killed the group's leader Hassan Nasrallah, senior commanders and thousands of its militants, Iran's Revolutionary Guards stepped in to help get its ally back on its feet ahea...

stories.title: US deploys more troops to Mideast as Trump considers ‘winding down’ operations
stories.description: President Donald Trump said his administration was considering “winding down” military operations in the Middle East even as the United States announced it was sending more warships and Marines to the region and Iran threatened to attack tourist sites worldwide.

stories.title: Iranian woman’s video of US-Israel attack ends as bomb hits
stories.description: The fate of an Iranian woman caught in a US-Israeli bomb attack is unknown, after video emerged of her filming it.

stories.title: Unease in Japan after Trump cites Pearl Harbor to defend Iran war
stories.description: US president's reference to Japan's 1941 attack has shaken Japanese as PM Takaichi's silence gets mixed reaction.

stories.title: Iran says US and Israel attacked Natanz nuclear facility
stories.description: No leakage of radioactive materials reported in the area in central Iran, Tehran's atomic energy organisation says.

stories.title: War spirals as information control tightens
stories.description: From Washington to Tehran to Tel Aviv, the fight to control the narrative seems as critical as the war itself.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 22 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: As conflict enters week 4, here’s the latest on the US-Israel attack on Iran and across the Middle East.

stories.title: Iran says it will allow Japanese ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Japan sources 90 percent of its crude from Middle East and is heavily dependent on exports transiting the key waterway.

stories.title: Trump hints at ‘winding down’ Iran war as US deploys more troops to region
stories.description: Trump says he is not looking for a ceasefire with Iran as mixed messages from US president come amid an escalating war.

stories.title: Iran war live: US-Israel attack Natanz nuclear site; Diego Garcia targeted
stories.description: Trump says Strait of Hormuz must be protected from Iranian attacks 'by other nations who use it'.

stories.title: Pezeshkian says Iran not seeking war with Muslim neighbours
stories.description: Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian says his country is not seeking war with its neighbours.

stories.title: India cooking gas crisis forces exodus of textile workers
stories.description: Indian textile workers are returning home after days without cooking gas, as an LPG crisis linked to the Iran war.

stories.title: Military movements indicate Trump is considering Iran ground operation
stories.description: The US is moving military assets to the Middle East that are key to providing support for ground troop operations.

stories.title: Palestinian citizens of Israel demand better protection against missiles
stories.description: At least 15 people have been killed by attacks in Israel since the war on Iran started late last month.

stories.title: Trump says considering ‘winding down’ Iran war but rules out ceasefire
stories.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that the United States was considering “winding down” military operations against Iran but was not seeking a ceasefire with the Islamic Republic. Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei meanwhile, claimed in a message to mark the first day of...

stories.title: Dubai property sector shows early signs of weakness
stories.description: Dubai’s property market is beginning to show early signs of weakening nearly three weeks into ​the US-Israeli war on Iran, with data from analysts showing tanking transaction volumes and some real estate agents pointing to price reductions. The war, and ‌Tehran’s strikes against ...

stories.title: US stocks skid as Middle East turmoil fans inflation fear
stories.description: Wall Street ended sharply lower on Friday, with the S&P 500 closing at its lowest in six months, as the US-Israeli war against Iran entered its fourth week, deepening worries about inflation and the potential for higher interest rates. The conflict in the Middle East showed no si...

stories.title: Supreme Leader Mojtaba denies Iran’s role in attacks in Oman, Turkiye, dubs them ‘false flag tactic’
stories.description: Iran’s supreme leader, Mojataba Khamenei, denied on Friday Tehran’s role in recent attacks in Turkiye and Oman, claiming that it was a “false flag tactic” by Israel instead. “I should also remark that the attacks against Turkiye and Oman – both of which have good relations with u...

stories.title: Gold falls 1.8pc after report of US sending more troops to Middle East
stories.description: Gold prices fell by 1.8 per cent on Friday as the dollar strengthened on a report that the United States will deploy extra troops in the Middle East, fanning concerns of higher oil prices, inflation, and with it, elevated interest rates. Spot gold fell 1.8pc to $4,563.64 per ounc...

stories.title: Israel carries out strikes on missile production facilities in Tehran — army statement
stories.description: According to the IDF, dozens of targets were hit, including a central compound of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps

stories.title: Consequences of US-Israeli aggression against Iran to last for long time — Lavrov
stories.description: According to the top Russian diplomat, although some say that this time, history is being repeated as a farce, "it’s far from being a farce"

stories.title: US strikes more than 8,000 targets in Iran, including 130 ships — Pentagon
stories.description: Cooper also noted that "US forces maintain air superiority over Iran's skies, having now flown over 8,000 combat flights"

stories.title: Intensity of Israeli-US attacks on Iran to increase drastically — Israeli defense minister
stories.description: The Israel Defense Forces are strong, and the Israeli home front is strong, and we will not stop until all military goals are achieved, Katz added

stories.title: IRGC announces start of 71st wave of attacks on Israel, US regional bases
stories.description: In a statement released by the Mehr agency, the IRGC declared that the strikes are targeting Tel Aviv, the US military bases Ali Al Salem in Kuwait and Al Kharij in Saudi Arabia

stories.title: Iran informs IAEA of strike on Natanz facility, no leaks recorded — statement
stories.description: Earlier, the Tasnim agency reported that US and Israeli forces had attacked the Iranian nuclear facility in Natanz, and no leaks of radioactive materials were recorded

stories.title: Putin congratulates heads of CIS countries, Iranian leaders on Islamic holiday of Nowruz
stories.description: International Nowruz Day, established by the United Nations in 2010, is celebrated on March 21, marking the vernal equinox and the beginning of the new year for Iranian and Turkic peoples

stories.title: Iran confirms attack on Diego Garcia island with ballistic missiles
stories.description: According to its information, this launch demonstrates a "significant step" in the confrontation with the United States

stories.title: Central Israel comes under new missile fire from Iran, explosions heard in Tel Aviv area
stories.description: An air alert was declared in many areas of the center of the country, with sirens sounding, including in Tel Aviv and its suburbs, a TASS correspondent reported

stories.title: Ship disguised as broken vessel passed through Strait of Hormuz — agency
stories.description: According to its information, the Jamal first appeared on radar on March 13 in the Gulf of Oman, with its previous location remaining unknown

stories.title: UN could help resolve shipping issue in Strait of Hormuz — Secretary-General
stories.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced a retaliatory operation, targeting sites in Israel. US military bases in Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the UAE were also hit

stories.title: Iran ready to allow Japanese ships to pass through Strait of Hormuz — foreign minister
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi says that the strait is open

stories.title: Israel’s strikes on Tehran target Iranian Police Chief — TV
stories.description: Iranian officials have not commented on the report

stories.title: US lifts sanctions against two Russians — Treasury Department
stories.description: The restrictions were discontinued for Yury Korzhavin and Lidia Korzhavina

stories.title: Capitalization of global airlines loses $53 bln due to situation around Iran — FT
stories.description: The article says that investors expect the share price decrease to continue

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
